message:
perf(tree-shake): add runtime module tree shaking for smaller bundles

Split runtime.js into 15 modular files in src/polyfills/runtime/:
- core.js: console, timers, error handling (always included)
- base64.js, text.js, events.js, crypto.js, streams.js, url.js
- abort.js, fetch.js, buffer.js, wasm.js, host.js, tsc.js, etc.

Added analyzeGlobals() to tree_shake.zig to detect global API usage
(fetch, Buffer, URL, etc.) and include only required runtime modules.

Results:
- Hello world: 123KB → 52KB bundle (58% reduction)
- With fetch: 123KB → 69KB (44% reduction)
- With Buffer: 123KB → 61KB (50% reduction)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+// EdgeBox Runtime: End block
+// Closes the initialization guard opened in core.js
+
+// Mark runtime polyfills as initialized
+globalThis._runtimePolyfillsInitialized = true;
+
+} // End of guard block from core.js
